mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing the present value of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate profit expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, which expands corporate revenue streams and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with 60-day forward returns showing a negative or zero mean during concurrent RSAFS and HOUST expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations during identified post-shock recovery periods, or the strategy Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 50 percent of high-inflation months, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL spikes and equity declines is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeding 2 percent signals sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of late-cycle quarters with GDP growth above 2 percent, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1 percent indicates contracting manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, which precipitates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances following a 1 percent three-month industrial production decline, or the signal hit rate falls below 45 percent out-of-sample.
